layman_headline: Crypto market gripped by extreme fear — no clear safe entry exists right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is in a state of extreme fear, with the Fear & Greed index at just 25 out of 100 and worsening. Bitcoin is holding relatively steady but its dominance at 56.5% means money is flowing away from altcoins. Every swing trade candidate on the watchlist shows bearish chart patterns — selling pressure is real across the board. While extreme fear can eventually create great buying opportunities, there's no clear reversal signal yet to justify jumping in.
layman_action: Stay in cash and be patient. Watch for a fear & greed recovery above 30 and for key support levels to hold before committing capital. The market needs to show it wants to bounce before you buy the dip.
expert_reasoning: All nine swing candidates show bearish or strongly bearish chart structures. BONK has the most oversold RSI (30.75) but its chart is catastrophically bearish (-2.0 score) with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — catching that knife would be reckless. ONDO has the only positive chart bias (+0.40) but RSI at 62 suggests it's already extended after a 19.7% 5-day move, and the risk/reward to nearest levels is terrible at 0.38x. ETH is the best risk-adjusted candidate with RSI at 39 (approaching oversold — our highest-confidence signal type at 80% win rate), but it remains below all moving averages. The prudent play is to wait for ETH to test and hold $1,800 support, then enter on confirmation. BTC dominance at 56.5% is actively hostile to alt positions, and the worsening fear trend (25 and declining) suggests we haven't seen capitulation yet.
risk_factors: Extreme fear at 25 and worsening — sentiment could deteriorate further with weekend geopolitical risk around oil markets
BTC dominance at 56.5% actively draining liquidity from altcoins, making alt swing trades structurally disadvantaged
Every candidate has bearish chart structure — there is no confirmed reversal pattern anywhere on the watchlist
watch_list: ETH holding $1,800 support — a decisive break below would invalidate the oversold bounce thesis
Fear & Greed index — watch for stabilization or uptick above 30 as a potential sentiment floor signal
BTC reaction to weekend geopolitical news and whether $64K holds — massive call spreads targeting $72K suggest institutional optimism that hasn't materialized yet
market_context: The crypto market is in extreme fear (25/100, worsening) with BTC holding ~$64.4K while altcoins bleed. High BTC dominance at 56.5% means capital is rotating out of alts, making swing trades in anything besides BTC particularly risky right now.
context_banner: Extreme fear dominates with universally bearish alt charts — capital preservation is the priority until a clear reversal signal emerges.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 25 (Extreme Fear) and worsening from 27 yesterday — no sentiment floor established yet, which historically precedes further downside before a bounce.
ETH RSI at 39.4 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal type (80% win rate) — but hasn't crossed below 30 to confirm full oversold conditions.
Every candidate except ONDO has negative chart bias scores; BONK at -2.0 with Three Black Crows confirms aggressive selling across alts.
Massive BTC call spreads targeting $72K by month-end coinciding with Fed meeting could catalyze a broader market rally, but hasn't triggered yet.
Weekend geopolitical risk with 20% of global oil supply in the balance adds macro uncertainty that could deepen the fear cycle.
